sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during recess:
 Approving plans and specifications for the FY 2025 Curb Ramp Improvement
Project;
 Accepting the bid of Rosas Brothers Construction as the lowest, responsive and
responsible bidder; and
 Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions, or other change orders until project completion, in accordance with
the approved plans and specifications in an amount not to exceed $2,683,395,
which includes a 10% contingency.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
Delay in approving this contract would postpone critical accessibility improvements that
must be completed before the FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project.
This would risk pushing paving work outside the optimal weather window and potentially
delay the entire street paving program, impacting multiple project schedules and
increasing costs.
sections.fiscal_impacts:

Measure BB Sales Tax (Fund 134) ............................................................$55,500
Capital Improvement Fund (Fund 501)..................................................$2,194,500
Total construction cost $2,683,395
No other funding is required, and no other projects will be delayed due to this expenditure.

sections.current_situation:
The project incorporates 108 curb ramp improvements at locations corresponding to the
streets selected for the FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project (see
Attachment 2, Location Map for Curb Ramp Improvements). Completing the curb ramp
improvements before the FY 2025 Street Maintenance and Rehabilitation Project will
ensure the timely completion of the paving within the optimal weather window.
The project was released for bids on October 3, 2024, and bids were opened on October
24, 2024 (see Attachment 3, Abstract of Bids). Seven non-local/local bids were received,
from a low of $2,439,450 to a high of $2,942,450. The engineer’s estimate for the project
was $2,600,000.
Rosas Brothers Construction of Oakland, California, was the lowest, responsive and
responsible bidder, with a bid of $2,439,450, and their references were satisfactory. This
project includes a 10% contingency, which brings the total contract amount up to the
requested $2,683,395. Staff recommends that a contract for this project be awarded to
Rosas Brothers Construction.
The FY 2025 Curb Ramp Improvement Project is a Strategic Plan Priority Project,
advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities,
and facilities.
sections.background:
The City is required to provide accessible curb ramps under Title II of the Americans with
Disabilities Act (ADA) and Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973. Curb ramps are
a critical connection between sidewalks and street crossings, enabling people with
disabilities, seniors, parents with strollers, and all pedestrians to navigate our city safely.
When streets are altered through resurfacing projects, federal law requires the
simultaneous installation or upgrading curb ramps to current ADA standards at affected
crosswalk locations. The City has been systematically upgrading curb ramps throughout
Berkeley as part of its ongoing commitment to accessibility and compliance with federal
regulations. The FY 2025 Curb Ramp Improvement Project will upgrade 108 curb ramps
to meet current standards for slope, width, surface texture, and other accessibility
requirements.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This project supports the City's climate action goals by improving pedestrian
infrastructure and encouraging walking as an alternative to driving. The improvements

will also enhance accessibility for all residents, promoting a more equitable and
sustainable transportation system.
sections.rationale:
The City conducted a public bid process, and Rosas Brothers Construction submitted the
lowest, responsive, and responsible bid. The work requires contracted services, as the
City does not possess the in-house labor or equipment resources necessary to complete
this specialized construction work.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative action was considered. The City could choose not to proceed with this
project. However, this would prevent the City from improving accessibility and meeting its
obligations under the Americans with Disabilities Act. It could also delay the yearly Paving
project, which is part of the 5-Year Paving plan.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, (510) 981-6396
Ronald A. Nevels, Manager of Engineering, (510) 981-6439

Page 3
Page 4 of 6
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: ROSAS BROTHERS CONSTRUCTION FOR FY 2025 CURB RAMP
IMPROVEMENT PROJECT
WHEREAS, the FY 2025 Curb Ramp Improvement Project is part of the City’s ongoing
effort to enhance accessibility throughout the City; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
FY 2025 Curb Ramp Improvement Project; and
WHEREAS, an invitation for bids was duly advertised, and Rosas Brothers Construction
was the lowest responsive and responsible bidder; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the FY 2025 and FY 2026 budgets in Measure B Local
Streets & Roads (Fund 130), Measure BB Sales Tax (Fund 134), and Capital
Improvement Fund (Fund 501); and
WHEREAS, during the Council recess period from December 11, 2024 through January
20, 2025, the City Manager took action to approve the contract to maintain project
schedules and meet accessibility requirements.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager to approve plans and specifications
for Specification No. 25-11679-C FY 2025 Curb Ramp Improvement Project.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley ratifies the City
Manager's action accepting the bid and executing a contract and any amendments,
extensions or change orders until completion of the project in accordance with the
approved plans and specifications with Rosas Brothers Construction for the FY 2025 Curb
Ramp Improvement Project, in an amount not to exceed $2,683,395, which includes a
10% contingency for unforeseen circumstances. A record signature copy of said
agreement and any amendments are to be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
